content: {"perspective": "Industry", "question": "Round 2 revealed that the JMIR 52-week LIFE app study showed 13.9% weight loss in BMI≥40 users but slight weight GAIN in lower-BMI users. Two industry questions follow: (1) What self-reported adverse events, medical complications, or concerning patterns — hypoglycemia, fainting, eating disorder relapses, electrolyte disturbances, reported hospitalizations — have been documented in the real-world IF app literature or post-market surveillance by commercial IF app vendors? (2) Has any IF app adjusted its marketing, onboarding screening, or protocol recommendations in response to the BMI-dependent benefit pattern, or are they still marketed uniformly to all users regardless of baseline BMI or metabolic risk?", "search_queries": ["intermittent fasting app adverse event real-world safety hypoglycemia eating disorder relapse", "Zero Fastic Simple fasting app medical complication hospitalization safety surveillance", "intermittent fasting app onboarding screening BMI contraindication eligibility eating disorder", "commercial fasting app marketing 2024 BMI dependent benefit weight loss eligibility", "intermittent fasting eating disorder risk binge subclinical app real world cohort"]}
